page.title: Day 10: Sharks, Rays, and Sea Turtles

page:
# Day 10: Sharks, Rays, and Sea Turtles
## Focus
Elasmobranch sensory biology, sea turtle navigation, and conservation policy.
## Learning Objectives
- Describe sensory adaptations in sharks and rays. - Explain key features of sea turtle life history and nesting behavior. - Evaluate how fishing pressure and policy shape species outcomes.
## Core Topics
### Sharks and Rays
Elasmobranchs use specialized senses, including electroreception, to detect prey and navigate marine environments.
### Sea Turtles
Sea turtles depend on nesting beaches, oceanic migration routes, and developmental habitats that may span multiple jurisdictions.
### Conservation Challenges
Bycatch, habitat loss, pollution, illegal harvest, and finning create management problems that require both biological knowledge and policy coordination.
## Applied Activity
Complete a final comparison chart:
- marine mammals - sharks and rays - sea turtles
For each, note one adaptation, one threat, and one effective conservation response.
## Final Reflection Setup
Use today’s content to choose a real-world marine issue for your reflection essay. Your final submission should connect course concepts across physical systems, ecology, organismal biology, and conservation.
